summary: The suite covers most stated requirements: glob/recursive search, preview with context and replacement preview, backup creation with content verification, summary report structure, capture-group regex, skipping unmodified files, and the three main error paths (bad path, empty pattern, invalid regex). Key gap: preview mode is never asserted to leave files unmodified — the core guarantee of 'preview' is untested. Backup absence when -CreateBackup is omitted is also unverified. Rigor is moderate: happy paths and a few error cases are solid, but edge cases are largely absent — matches at the very first or last line of a file (context boundary clamping), empty files, files with a single line, multiple regex matches on the same line, and whitespace/special characters in paths are all untested. Design is readable with clear Arrange/Act/Assert comments and GUID-based temp dirs ensuring independence, but the 'no matches' test makes two redundant calls and adds a confusing inline comment about [array] coercion instead of just testing correctly. Error-handling tests manage their own BeforeEach/AfterEach inline rather than using the Describe-level hooks used elsewhere, creating an inconsistency. The Set-Content with a backtick-n joined string could produce platform-dependent line splitting that makes the multiline context test fragile.
